reviews.review_description: I like this game. The only issue for me is when playing multiplayer in Mexican restaurant, I can't attach the fruit to the glass. So basically non playable in multiplayer. I really hate that because I like that restaurant.

Clash of Chefs VR is a culinary powerhouse that delivers depth, execution, and pure fun across three modes: endless, missions, and chilled practice. With five unique restaurants that ramp up in difficulty, mastering every dish while keeping pace is a thrilling challenge.
The game’s standout feature? The ability to focus on one cuisine at a time, making progression feel organic and satisfying. And when you’re ready to team up or compete, the multiplayer options shine—whether you’re racing in PVP or working together in co-op to hit the highest ratings.
Well-polished, packed with content, and endlessly replayable, Clash of Chefs VR is a must-play for cooking game fans. Highly recommended! 🍽️🎮

Bought this game for myself, husband and daughter to play together since it said multi-player.  Unfortunately  2 is the limit. I wish it would specify what multi-player means exactly.  Game is fun and challenging but we hate leaving one of us out.

I really like this game, however, we are not able to get orders in Chinese and Japanese when playing pvp.  Only one person is getting orders.  Also, the cheese grater gets hung up under the shelf near the trash bin.  Please correct.
O love the Chinese version but I doesn’t show how to fixed the noodles portion I was confused on how to fix please help.
This game is fire but I have experienced some bugs but it’s okay 👌 like the game is nostalgic so  so cool
This cooking game can be intense and gives you a work out if your moving the way I do...lol
It gives you a code to allow quick game joining but it’s not allowing for us to join with code in co op setting

The game is incredibly fun to play and polished and the fact that the developers keep adding more updates even after 3 years makes it even better! My favorite is the Italian kitchen though, so fun making pizzas! Really hoping I'll see a co-op mode soon 🙏
The new Chinese food addition has a bug where the waiter is stuck walking into your station and does not give any orders during pvp play.

developer_response.body: Hi, can you let us know what is happening so that we can reproduce the bug and fix it.  You can write to us at contact@flathillgames.com. Thanks,

reviews.review_title: Hey love ur game I have some ideas
reviews.review_description:
I love ur game it so fun and amazing but I think u should add more places for food like Taiwan food like boba, shaved ice, dumplings, and hot pots 
And maybe Chinese like egg rolls, spring rolls and chow mein and others like Korean food like Korean corn dogs, Kimchi, gimbamp and Tanghulu and lastly French food like croissants, baguettes, crepes, macarons, and soufflés and if u can can u add mochi to the japenese reasturant please thank you and again these are suggestions but otherwise I love the game it’s so fun.
Great game!
I love this game I could play it for hours! But it would be good if you could add more food to the American menu like hot dogs? As it’s just burgers and fries on that menu.

This game is very entertaining but has room for improvement.

At first, it is quite fun, highly realistic, and detailed. The graphics and mechanics create an immersive experience that keeps players engaged. However, there are some aspects that could be enhanced to make the gameplay even better.

Suggestions for the Developers:

Add More Kitchens: Expanding the variety of kitchen environments would keep the gameplay fresh and exciting. Different settings with unique challenges could make the game even more immersive.

Introduce a Cooperative Mode: Allowing players to work together would make the experience more dynamic and enjoyable. A multiplayer feature could enhance teamwork and replayability.

Improve the Seated Gameplay Experience: While the counter is adjustable, playing while seated is not very comfortable. Optimizing the game for seated play would make it more accessible for all players.


Overall, the game has a great foundation, but with these improvements, it could become an even better experience for all players.

reviews.review_title: One of the better cooking games
reviews.review_description: This is one of the better cooking games I played. I usually play unlimited/casual which is nice with no competition or time crunch. I would love more cooking styles, like holiday themes. Halloween concoctions, Thanksgiving feasts, Christmas deserts would be fun
I love playing against others and there is never anyone to play with
It’s a great game but a coop mode would make it even better
If you like cooking games you need this one! I personally think this is by far the best cooking game on vr, you can do solo or against people! so much to do and just so fun
As a family we really like it its so fun to play

I love this game but I don’t have anyone to play with and every time I try multiplayer I’m assuming no ones online. I’d love to play with other people other VR computer! 

Feel free to add me on the VR so we could compete!

Don’t get me wrong I really love this game, it’s one of the best cooking games out there. And everything that do they do great 

It’s jsut a shame this game has been abandoned but eh looks of it, no new kitchens since launch, and after 3 years the game is just stale and repetitive 

But on sale
You should make the game were you work together  and not against each other like overcooked 2

reviews.review_title: Quickly became one of my faves
reviews.review_description: I knew it would be fun when I watched the gameplay on youtube, and I was not wrong! It can get quite addictive and you'll easily spend hours playing this game. Being able to play with everything I need within an arm's reach is great because I still get nauseous on games where I am required to walk or run. This game does it for me! Hope to see more from this or a similar fun game from you guys.
Yall need to add more cuisines to da list
Love this game so much fun. My favorite VR game.
Nuggets are good
Like the game but it keeps getting stuck where I have to stop and restart

developer_response.body: Hi Anghela76, we are sorry to hear that you are experiencing this. Thank you for letting us know. Please send us an email to contact@flathillgames.com, so we are able to take a closer look at this issue.

reviews.review_title: Fun but needs a little love
reviews.review_description:
Love the premise of the game, and when it works, it’s a lot of fun.  For us, every time a level loads, the controllers do not register and you can’t do anything at all.  After moving frantically, the will appear again, sometimes disappearing halfway through a level.  

This makes it impossible to play.

You do have to get use to it at first, but it is SO much fun!!

Could I request a trash bin, please??
This game was bad in general
I have read other reviews about how it is glitchy and not very good but I have not experienced any of those yet. Absolutely amazing game and will continue to play the game.
Need more food and boards 😩😩 I’m addicted lol

My kids and I really love this one. So far, at least. It plays just like diner dash, but more realistic. The only issue so far is that in the sushi restaurant we can't get the rice to work.  No matter what we do, it stays on a ball and we can't do anything with it. If someone could get that glitch fixed, we would give 5 stars! 

Edit: We tried uninstalling then Re-installing it. It worked! No more complaints about the game, and raised it to 5 stars.

developer_response.body: Hi RebeccatheNomad, we are sorry to hear this! There are video tutorials in our game at the beginning of the level and also in In-game and Main menu when you select Options and then click on Tutorial button. You can surely check them out if you are having trouble with how to make things done! Nori and rice should be placed on the bamboo roller on your left side so they can stick and you can roll it that way. If you are still having issues, please send us an email to contact@flathillgames.com, so we are able to take a closer look or send you some specific instructions if needed. Thank you for reaching out!
